nodes.name: San Francisco
nodes.summary: A city in California that has implemented a universal basic income pilot program.

nodes.name: Universal Basic Income Pilot Program
nodes.summary: A program providing $1,000 per month to 1,000 randomly selected residents for two years.

nodes.name: Residents
nodes.summary: 1,000 randomly selected individuals receiving $1,000 per month for two years.

nodes.name: Business Groups
nodes.summary: Organizations that have announced plans to challenge the tax in court.

nodes.name: Commercial Real Estate Transactions
nodes.summary: Transactions above $5 million that will be taxed at 0.5% to fund the program.

nodes.name: Tax on Commercial Real Estate Transactions
nodes.summary: A 0.5% tax on transactions above $5 million to fund the universal basic income pilot program.

nodes.name: Housing Affordability Crisis
nodes.summary: A severe crisis in San Francisco where median rent exceeds $3,500 per month.

nodes.name: Court
nodes.summary: The entity where business groups plan to challenge the tax.
